nodes.name: Red-collared babbler
nodes.summary: The red-collared babbler (Turdoides rufocinctus), also known as the red-collared mountain-babbler, is a passerine bird in the family Leiothrichidae. It is native to the Albertine Rift montane forests. It is threatened by habitat loss. The red-collared babbler was moved from the genus Kupeornis to Turdoides based on the results of a molecular phylogenetic study published in 2018. References Collar, N. J. & Robson, C. 2007. Family Timaliidae (Babblers) pp. 70 – 291 in; del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A. & Christie, D.A. eds. Handbook of the Birds of the World, Vol. 12. Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona.

nodes.summary: Turdoides（Turdoides）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联19种鸟类，如Capuchin babbler、White-throated mountain babbler、Chapin's babbler等19种鸟类。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: Albertine Rift montane forests
nodes.summary: Albertine Rift montane forests是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联44种鸟类，包括Barred long-tailed cuckoo、白枕鸽、Albertine owlet等44种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。
